entities.name: ROOT ZONE PLANT-AVAILABLE WATER HOLDING CAPACITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil root zone to hold water available for plant use.

entities.name: CROP GROWTH
entities.description: The process of a crop growing from seed to maturity.

entities.name: YIELD RESPONSE
entities.description: The change in crop yield resulting from management inputs.

entities.name: IMPROVED SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds developed for better yield or stress tolerance.

entities.name: FERTILISERS
entities.description: Chemical or organic substances added to soil to increase fertility.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The region of Africa south of the Sahara desert.

entities.name: ROOTABLE DEPTH
entities.description: The depth of the soil profile that is accessible to plant roots.

entities.name: DIGITAL SOIL MAPPING
entities.description: Techniques used to produce spatially explicit maps of soil properties.

entities.name: PEDOTRANSFER FUNCTIONS
entities.description: Functions used to estimate soil hydraulic properties from more easily measurable soil properties.

entities.name: FIELD CAPACITY
entities.description: The amount of soil moisture held in soil after excess water has drained away.

entities.name: WILTING POINT
entities.description: The minimum soil moisture level at which plants can no longer extract water.

entities.name: SOIL FINE EARTH FRACTION
entities.description: The portion of soil consisting of particles smaller than 2mm.

entities.name: COARSE FRAGMENTS CONTENT
entities.description: The proportion of soil consisting of particles larger than 2mm.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal grain crop used to evaluate soil rootability and rootable depth.

entities.name: SOIL ROOTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of roots to penetrate the soil relative to threshold values.

entities.name: PEARSON CORRELATION
entities.description: A statistical measure of the linear correlation between two variables.

entities.name: DEPTH OF AERATION
entities.description: A soil condition that limits root zone depth based on oxygen availability.

entities.name: SODICITY
entities.description: The presence of excess sodium in the soil, limiting root zone depth.

entities.name: DEPTH TO BEDROCK
entities.description: The distance from the soil surface to the underlying bedrock, limiting rootable volume.

entities.name: ALUMINIUM TOXICITY
entities.description: High levels of soluble aluminium in soil that inhibit root growth.

entities.name: POROSITY
entities.description: The measure of void spaces in a material, affecting rootable soil volume.

entities.name: ALKALINITY
entities.description: The basicity of the soil, which can restrict root zone depth.
